interviewStages: Behavioral: customer empathy (construction industry has unique needs), collaboration
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design a multi-tenant project management system with role-based permissions
Design document management with version control, e-signatures, and audit trails
Design integrations with hundreds of third-party tools (Excel, Bluebeam, Sage)

coding: At Procore, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Procore scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer empathy (construction industry has unique needs), collaboration.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a multi-tenant project management system with role-based permissions.
Design document management with version control, e-signatures, and audit trails.
Design integrations with hundreds of third-party tools (Excel, Bluebeam, Sage).
values: Procore succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: What is the construction industry like as a domain?
How does Procore compare to Autodesk Construction Cloud?

rawSummary:
Procore Interview Guide (2026): Construction Tech Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Procore
procore.com ↗

Procore is the leading construction management platform — used by 16,000+ general contractors and subcontractors. The interview focuses on enterprise SaaS engineering, complex multi-tenant data modeling, and the realities of building software for an industry that values reliability over novelty.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral, 1 hiring manager. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a multi-tenant project management system with role-based permissions

- Design document management with version control, e-signatures, and audit trails

- Design integrations with hundreds of third-party tools (Excel, Bluebeam, Sage)

- Coding: medium DSA, often with data-modeling framing

- Behavioral: customer empathy (construction industry has unique needs), collaboration

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $145K–$180K total

- Senior SE: $210K–$280K

- Staff: $300K–$400K

- Principal: $420K–$550K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Ruby on Rails (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand enterprise SaaS patterns: SSO, SCIM, audit logs, RBAC

- Read about modular monolith architecture (Procore is a famous example)

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Procore remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Carpinteria (HQ), Austin, NYC, and others. Many engineering roles are fully remote within US.

What is the construction industry like as a domain?

Friendly, demanding, and conservative on tech adoption. Customers value reliability over novelty. Engineers benefit from understanding construction workflows.

How does Procore compare to Autodesk Construction Cloud?

Procore is dedicated; Autodesk has BIM/design integration. Procore pays slightly less but moves faster on product features.
